free_text: Systematic investigation of 1.2-GeV proton-induced
spallation reactions on targets between Al and U.
More
details of experiment.
The experiments utilised the 4pi-detector NESSI
(Neutron Scintillator tank and Silicon ball) for an
efficient and simultaneous detection of neutrons and
charged particles.  In addition to the Berlin Neutron
Ball (BNB), and the Berlin Silicon Ball (BSiB), the
setup has been equipped with six threefold delta-E-E
telescopes. Each telescope  consists  of  a stack of
three delta-E Si detectors with thicknesses of 25-27
mum, 80-90 mum and approximately 1000 mum, backed by a
7-cm thick CsI(Tl) crystal of 4 cm in diameter.
The Cooler Synchrotron Facility COSY.
Particle identification with  BSiB  detectors was
obtained by means of energy and time-of-flight
measurements;
For the Si/CsI(Tl) telescopes.
Results of simulation calculations with the
intra-nuclear cascade code INCL2.0 coupled to the
statistical model GEMINI.
The cross sections are corrected for the counting
losses.
SB
SD: SF4=NPART -> 0-NN-1; SF7=N deleted and
SF8=RAW added in REACTION code in Subents 2-11
according MEMO CP-C/406.
SD: Correction in several Subents.
fig 2
see CORRECTION
Not corrected for the neutron energy dependent
efficiency.
The following corrections have been applied to the
measured distributions-
-the portion of background from reaction outside the
target 1-2%,or He4.
-events rejected by the double reactions inspection
2-4% are accounted for, continuously monitored during
the runs,
-An off-line threshold was introduced into the
measured BNB-light spectrum at 10 MeV to cut events
from electrons observed at M(n) less than 3. The
extrapolation to zero energy was estimated assuming
similar spectra shapes as measured for M(n)=3-5,
-A small portion of elastically scattered beam protons
may enter the BNB scintillator thus simulating real
events. This background of about 1-3% contributes to
the peak observed in the BNB-light spectrum which is
well pronounced for M(n) less or equal 5 at energies
of about 50 MeV.
A thin targets, thickness 0.1-1 g/cm**2 were used.
.By CAJAD. Digitized from fig.2 of NIM/A,562,729
.Digitizing error of data.
